title: Doric Weekly Market Insight
summary:
"With western economies lacking vividness, Baltic indices haven't yet set a clear course and continued trading within a very narrow range the last couple of months, with the local peaks and troughs being less than three thousand dollars apart."
By Michalis Voutsinas
Following last week's upbeat Chinese GDP data, economists are optimistic about Beijing hitting its full-year growth target of 5 percent in 2023. The higher-than-expected growth rate in the first quarter was partly driven by a rebound in consumption, with official data showing retail sales gathering momentum. In fact, retail sales in March rose a forecast-beating 10.6 percent, closing in on a two-year high.
